source_paper_title: Unbiased Least Squares Regression via Averaged Stochastic Gradient Descent
context:
Section 1 (Introduction), page 2 (PDF page 2/33), paragraph discussing known \(O(1/k)\) bounds for \(\bar\theta_k\).

It is noteworthy that the constant behind their O-notation does not depend on the smallest eigenvalue \(\lambda_{\min}\) of \(H\), which can be very small. We are not aware of a better non-asymptotic bound on the expected excess risk of \(\bar\theta_k\) that does not depend on \(\lambda_{\min}\).
title: Non-asymptotic excess-risk bound for tail-averaged SGD in least squares with no dependence on \(\lambda_{\min}(H)\)
background_latex:
- Let \((x,y)\) be a square-integrable random vector in \(\mathbb{R}^d\times\mathbb{R}\), and let \(((x_t,y_t))_{t\ge 0}\) be i.i.d. copies of \((x,y)\).
- Define the least-squares risk \(L(\theta):=\tfrac12\,\mathbb{E}\big((y-x^\top\theta)^2\big)\) for \(\theta\in\mathbb{R}^d\).
- Let \(H:=\mathbb{E}(xx^\top)\) and assume \(H\) is positive definite, with smallest eigenvalue \(\lambda_{\min}(H)>0\).
- Let \(\theta^*\) denote the unique minimizer of \(L\), equivalently the unique solution of \(H\theta^*=\mathbb{E}(yx)\).
- Consider constant-step-size stochastic gradient descent (SGD)
\[\theta_{t+1}=\theta_t-\gamma\,(x_t^\top\theta_t-y_t)x_t,\qquad t\ge 0,\]
with deterministic initialization \(\theta_0\in\mathbb{R}^d\) and step size \(\gamma>0\).
- For an integer \(k\ge 1\) and a burn-in \(s(k)\in\{0,1,\dots,k-1\}\), define the tail-averaged (time-averaged) iterate
\[\bar\theta_k:=\frac{1}{k-s(k)}\sum_{t=s(k)}^{k-1}\theta_t.\]
- The expected excess risk of \(\bar\theta_k\) is \(\mathbb{E}(L(\bar\theta_k))-L(\theta^*)\).
problem_definition_latex:
Prove a non-asymptotic upper bound of the form
\[\mathbb{E}(L(\bar\theta_k)) - L(\theta^*) \le \frac{C}{k}\]
for all integers \(k\ge 1\), where the constant \(C\) does not depend on the smallest eigenvalue \(\lambda_{\min}(H)\) (and in particular remains bounded as \(\lambda_{\min}(H)\to 0\)), under standard moment-type assumptions ensuring the recursion is well-defined.
latex:
- Let \((x,y)\) be a square-integrable random vector in \(\mathbb{R}^d\times\mathbb{R}\), and let \(((x_t,y_t))_{t\ge 0}\) be i.i.d. copies of \((x,y)\).
- Define the least-squares risk \(L(\theta):=\tfrac12\,\mathbb{E}\big((y-x^\top\theta)^2\big)\) for \(\theta\in\mathbb{R}^d\).
- Let \(H:=\mathbb{E}(xx^\top)\) and assume \(H\) is positive definite, with smallest eigenvalue \(\lambda_{\min}(H)>0\).
- Let \(\theta^*\) denote the unique minimizer of \(L\), equivalently the unique solution of \(H\theta^*=\mathbb{E}(yx)\).
- Consider constant-step-size stochastic gradient descent (SGD)
\[\theta_{t+1}=\theta_t-\gamma\,(x_t^\top\theta_t-y_t)x_t,\qquad t\ge 0,\]
with deterministic initialization \(\theta_0\in\mathbb{R}^d\) and step size \(\gamma>0\).
- For an integer \(k\ge 1\) and a burn-in \(s(k)\in\{0,1,\dots,k-1\}\), define the tail-averaged (time-averaged) iterate
\[\bar\theta_k:=\frac{1}{k-s(k)}\sum_{t=s(k)}^{k-1}\theta_t.\]
- The expected excess risk of \(\bar\theta_k\) is \(\mathbb{E}(L(\bar\theta_k))-L(\theta^*)\).

Prove a non-asymptotic upper bound of the form
\[\mathbb{E}(L(\bar\theta_k)) - L(\theta^*) \le \frac{C}{k}\]
for all integers \(k\ge 1\), where the constant \(C\) does not depend on the smallest eigenvalue \(\lambda_{\min}(H)\) (and in particular remains bounded as \(\lambda_{\min}(H)\to 0\)), under standard moment-type assumptions ensuring the recursion is well-defined.

We are not aware of a better non-asymptotic bound on the expected excess risk of \(\bar\theta_k\) that does not depend on \(\lambda_{\min}\).
